title: Try CatBoost on raw categorical interactions instead of manual frequency encoding
summary: The strongest public guidance for mixed tabular data with high-cardinality categories is to let CatBoost build ordered target statistics and category combinations.
rationale: The strongest public guidance for mixed tabular data with high-cardinality categories is to let CatBoost build ordered target statistics and category combinations. That is materially different from the current manual frequency-encoding approach and is designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoost with raw categorical columns (`Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, `Stint`) plus a compact numeric set. Test explicit combos such as `Race×Compound`, `Driver×Compound`, and `Year×Race` under the stricter grouped CV above. Keep preprocessing minimal; avoid manual one-hot for these columns.
expected_effect: Potential lift from better handling of rare categories, unseen combinations, and track-specific strategy effects.
risk: Benefits can disappear under weak CV; CatBoost defaults around one-hot thresholds and random seeds need controlled tuning.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
